startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: Greataxe and four handaxes
Option B: Any martial melee weapon and two handaxes
Option C: 75 GP

level1Features.name: Rage
level1Features.description: As a bonus action, enter a rage lasting 1 minute. While raging you have advantage on Strength checks and saves, +2 damage on melee attacks using Strength, and resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. You can rage 2 times per long rest at level 1.

level1Features.name: Unarmored Defense
level1Features.description: While not wearing armor your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit.

subclasses.name: Path of the Berserker
subclasses.description: The Path of the Berserker is a path of untrammeled fury, slick with blood. Berserkers follow a simple code: strength is everything.

features.name: Frenzy
features.description: You can go into a frenzy when you rage. If you do, for the duration of your rage you can make a single melee weapon attack as a bonus action on each of your turns. When your rage ends, you suffer one level of exhaustion.

features.name: Mindless Rage
features.description: At 6th level, you can't be charmed or frightened while raging. If you are charmed or frightened when you enter your rage, the effect is suspended for the duration of the rage.

subclasses.name: Path of the Totem Warrior
subclasses.description: The Path of the Totem Warrior is a spiritual journey as the barbarian accepts a spirit animal as guide, protector, and inspiration.

features.name: Spirit Seeker
features.description: You gain the ability to cast Beast Sense and Speak with Animals as rituals.

features.name: Totem Spirit
features.description: Choose a totem spirit: Bear (resistance to all damage except psychic while raging), Eagle (enemies have disadvantage on opportunity attacks against you and you can Dash as a bonus action while raging), or Wolf (your friends have advantage on melee attack rolls against hostile creatures adjacent to you while raging).

name: Bard
toolProficiencies: three musical instruments of your choice

startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: Leather armor, two daggers, and musical instrument
Option B: Studded leather armor, a scimitar, and musical instrument
Option C: 100 GP

level1Features.name: Bardic Inspiration
level1Features.description: As a bonus action, choose a creature other than yourself within 60 feet that can hear you. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die (d6 at level 1–4). Once within the next 10 minutes it can roll that die and add the result to an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw. You can use this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier per long rest.

level1Features.name: Spellcasting
level1Features.description: You can cast bard spells using Charisma as your spellcasting ability. You know 2 cantrips and 4 spells at level 1, and prepare new spells when you gain levels.

subclasses.name: College of Lore
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Lore know something about most things, collecting bits of knowledge from sources as diverse as scholarly tomes and peasant tales.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.

features.name: Cutting Words
features.description: When a creature you can see within 60 feet makes an attack roll, ability check, or damage roll, you can use your reaction to expend one Bardic Inspiration die to subtract the number rolled from the creature's roll.

subclasses.name: College of Valor
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Valor are daring skalds who keep alive the memory of great heroes and inspire a new generation.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons.

features.name: Combat Inspiration
features.description: A creature with a Bardic Inspiration die from you can also roll that die and add the number rolled to their AC against one attack or to a weapon damage roll.

startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: Chain mail, shield, and a mace
Option B: Scale mail, a mace, and light crossbow with 20 bolts
Option C: 110 GP

level1Features.name: Spellcasting
level1Features.description: You can cast cleric spells using Wisdom as your spellcasting ability. You prepare a number of spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + your cleric level. You know all cleric spells and choose which to prepare after each long rest.

level1Features.name: Divine Domain
level1Features.description: Choose a divine domain related to your deity. Your domain grants you domain spells and other features at 1st level and again at higher levels.

subclasses.name: Life Domain
subclasses.description: The Life domain focuses on the vibrant positive energy that sustains all life. Clerics of life are master healers who can restore the wounded and protect the living.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: You gain proficiency with heavy armor.

features.name: Disciple of Life
features.description: Your healing spells are more effective. Whenever you use a spell of 1st level or higher to restore hit points, the creature regains additional hit points equal to 2 + the spell's level.

subclasses.name: Light Domain
subclasses.description: Gods of light — including Helm, Lathander, Pholtus, Branchala, the Silver Flame, Belenus, Apollo, and Re-Horakhty — promote the ideals of rebirth and renewal.

features.name: Bonus Cantrip
features.description: You gain the Light cantrip if you don't already know it.

features.name: Warding Flare
features.description: When attacked, you can impose disadvantage on the attacker's roll as a reaction, interposing divine light. Usable a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier per long rest.

subclasses.name: Trickery Domain
subclasses.description: Gods of trickery are mischief-makers and instigators who stand as a constant challenge to the accepted order among both gods and mortals.

features.name: Blessing of the Trickster
features.description: You can use your action to give one willing creature (not yourself) advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. This blessing lasts for 1 hour or until you use this feature again.

subclasses.name: Knowledge Domain
subclasses.description: The gods of knowledge value learning and understanding above all else, and their clerics include sages, scholars, and librarians.

features.name: Blessings of Knowledge
features.description: You learn two languages of your choice and gain proficiency in two skills from Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for those two skills.

subclasses.name: Nature Domain
subclasses.description: Gods of nature are as varied as the natural world itself. Druids revere nature as a whole, while clerics of a nature deity focus on their deity's particular domain.

features.name: Acolyte of Nature
features.description: You learn one druid cantrip of your choice. You also gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Animal Handling, Nature, or Survival.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: You gain proficiency with heavy armor.

subclasses.name: Tempest Domain
subclasses.description: Gods whose portfolios include the Tempest domain govern storms, seas, and sky. Tempest clerics are fierce warriors who can call down the wrath of the heavens.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

features.name: Wrath of the Storm
features.description: When a creature within 5 feet hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to deal lightning or thunder damage equal to 2d8 to the attacker. Usable a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier per long rest.

subclasses.name: War Domain
subclasses.description: War has many manifestations. It can make heroes of ordinary people. It can be desperate and horrific, with acts of cruelty and cowardice eclipsing the deeds of the brave.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

features.name: War Priest
features.description: When you use the Attack action, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier per long rest.

startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: Leather armor, shield, and a wooden staff
Option B: Leather armor and a scimitar
Option C: 50 GP

level1Features.name: Druidic
level1Features.description: You know Druidic, the secret language of druids. You can speak it and use it to leave hidden messages. Creatures who know Druidic automatically spot such a message.

level1Features.name: Spellcasting
level1Features.description: You can cast druid spells using Wisdom as your spellcasting ability. You prepare spells after each long rest; the number you prepare equals your Wisdom modifier + your druid level.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Land
subclasses.description: The Circle of the Land is made up of mystics and sages who safeguard ancient knowledge and rites through a vast oral tradition.

features.name: Bonus Cantrip
features.description: You learn one additional druid cantrip of your choice.

features.name: Natural Recovery
features.description: Once per day after a short rest, you can recover expended spell slots with a combined level equal to half your druid level (rounded up). You cannot recover 6th-level or higher slots this way.

features.name: Circle Spells
features.description: Your mystical connection to the land infuses you with the ability to cast certain spells. Choose a type of terrain (Arctic, Coast, Desert, Forest, Grassland, Mountain, Swamp, or Underdark) to gain terrain-specific always-prepared spells.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Moon
subclasses.description: Druids of the Circle of the Moon are fierce guardians of the wilds who use Wild Shape as a primary weapon.

features.name: Combat Wild Shape
features.description: You can use Wild Shape as a bonus action. Additionally, while in beast form you can use a bonus action to expend spell slots to regain 1d8 hit points per spell slot level.

features.name: Circle Forms
features.description: You can use your Wild Shape to transform into beasts with a CR as high as 1 (at 2nd level) scaling upward. The CR limit increases as you gain druid levels.

startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: Chain mail, shield, and a martial weapon
Option B: Leather armor, longbow, 20 arrows, and a martial weapon
Option C: 175 GP

level1Features.name: Fighting Style
level1Features.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting. Choose Archery (+2 bonus to ranged attack rolls), Defense (+1 to AC when wearing armor), Dueling (+2 damage when wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons), Great Weapon Fighting (reroll 1s and 2s on damage dice for two-handed weapons), Protection (use reaction to impose disadvantage on attacks against an adjacent ally), or Two-Weapon Fighting (add ability modifier to the off-hand attack).

level1Features.name: Second Wind
level1Features.description: As a bonus action, regain 1d10 + your fighter level hit points. Usable once per short or long rest.

subclasses.name: Champion
subclasses.description: The archetypal Champion focuses on the development of raw physical power honed to deadly perfection.

features.name: Improved Critical
features.description: Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

features.name: Remarkable Athlete
features.description: At 7th level, you can add half your proficiency bonus (rounded up) to any Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution check you make that doesn't already use your proficiency bonus.

subclasses.name: Battle Master
subclasses.description: Those who emulate the archetypal Battle Master employ martial techniques passed down through generations.

features.name: Combat Superiority
features.description: You learn three maneuvers of your choice (from the Battle Master list) and gain four superiority dice (d8s). Spent dice are recovered on a short or long rest. Save DCs equal 8 + proficiency bonus + Strength or Dexterity modifier.

features.name: Student of War
features.description: You gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice.

subclasses.name: Eldritch Knight
subclasses.description: The archetypal Eldritch Knight combines the martial mastery common to all fighters with a careful study of magic.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You augment your martial prowess with the ability to cast spells. You learn two cantrips from the wizard spell list. You know 3 spells of 1st level, mostly from Abjuration and Evocation schools.

features.name: Weapon Bond
features.description: At 3rd level, you learn a ritual that creates a magical bond between yourself and one weapon. You can't be disarmed of a bonded weapon, and can summon it telekinetically as a bonus action.

name: Monk
toolProficiencies: one type of artisan's tools or one musical instrument

startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: A spear and five javelins
Option B: A short sword and a dagger
Option C: 50 GP

level1Features.name: Unarmored Defense
level1Features.description: While you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier.

level1Features.name: Martial Arts
level1Features.description: You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for attack and damage rolls with unarmed strikes and monk weapons. Your unarmed strikes deal 1d4 bludgeoning damage. When you use the Attack action with a monk weapon or unarmed strike, you can make one unarmed strike as a bonus action.

subclasses.name: Way of the Open Hand
subclasses.description: Monks of the Way of the Open Hand are the ultimate masters of martial arts combat, whether armed or unarmed.

features.name: Open Hand Technique
features.description: Whenever you hit a creature with one of the attacks granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects: the target must succeed on a Dexterity save or be knocked prone; the target must make a Strength save or be pushed up to 15 feet away; the target can't take reactions until the end of your next turn.

subclasses.name: Way of Shadow
subclasses.description: Monks of the Way of Shadow follow a tradition that values stealth and subterfuge.

features.name: Shadow Arts
features.description: You can spend 2 ki points to cast Darkness, Darkvision, Pass without Trace, or Silence without providing material components. You also know the Minor Illusion cantrip.

subclasses.name: Way of the Four Elements
subclasses.description: You follow a monastic tradition that teaches you to harness the elements, bending the four elements to your will.

features.name: Disciple of the Elements
features.description: You learn magical disciplines that use ki points. Disciplines include Breath of Winter, Clench of the North Wind, Eternal Mountain Defense, Fangs of the Fire Snake, Fist of Four Thunders, Fist of Unbroken Air, Flames of the Phoenix, Gong of the Summit, Mist Stance, Ride the Wind, River of Hungry Flame, Rush of the Gale Spirits, Shape of the Flowing River, Sweeping Cinder Strike, and Water Whip.

startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: Chain mail, shield, and a martial weapon
Option B: Scale mail, two handaxes, and a javelin
Option C: 150 GP

level1Features.name: Divine Sense
level1Features.description: As an action, open your awareness to detect celestials, fiends, and undead within 60 feet that are not behind total cover. You also detect consecrated or desecrated objects. Usable a number of times equal to 1 + your Charisma modifier per long rest.

level1Features.name: Lay on Hands
level1Features.description: A pool of healing power with a total of 5 × your paladin level hit points. As an action, touch a creature to restore any number of hit points from the pool. Alternatively, expend 5 points to cure one disease or neutralize one poison affecting the creature. Replenishes on long rest.

subclasses.name: Oath of Devotion
subclasses.description: The Oath of Devotion binds a paladin to the loftiest ideals of justice, virtue, and order. These paladins are the classic knights in shining armor.

features.name: Sacred Weapon
features.description: As an action, imbue one weapon with positive energy. For 1 minute, add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls with that weapon (minimum +1), it emits bright light in a 20-foot radius, and it counts as magical for overcoming resistance.

features.name: Turn the Unholy
features.description: As an action, present your holy symbol and speak a prayer censuring fiends and undead. Each such creature within 30 feet must make a Wisdom save. On a failure, the creature is turned for 1 minute.

subclasses.name: Oath of the Ancients
subclasses.description: The Oath of the Ancients is as old as the race of elves and the rituals of the druids. Paladins who swear this oath are called green knights.

features.name: Nature's Wrath
features.description: As an action, cause spectral vines to spring up and bind a creature within 10 feet. The creature must succeed on a Strength or Dexterity save (its choice) or be restrained. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns.

features.name: Turn the Faithless
features.description: As an action, present your holy symbol and speak a prayer castigating fey and fiends. Each such creature within 30 feet must make a Wisdom save or be turned for 1 minute.

subclasses.name: Oath of Vengeance
subclasses.description: The Oath of Vengeance is a solemn commitment to punish those who have committed a grievous sin. Paladins who uphold these tenets are called avengers.

features.name: Abjure Enemy
features.description: As an action, choose a creature and present your holy symbol. That creature must make a Wisdom save. On a failure, the creature is frightened for 1 minute and its speed is 0. On a success, its speed is halved.

features.name: Vow of Enmity
features.description: As a bonus action, utter a vow of enmity against a creature within 10 feet. You gain advantage on attack rolls against that creature for 1 minute, or until it drops to 0 hit points or falls unconscious.

startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: Scale mail, two shortswords, and a longbow with 20 arrows
Option B: Leather armor, a longsword, and a shortbow with 20 arrows
Option C: 150 GP

level1Features.name: Favored Enemy
level1Features.description: Choose a type of favored enemy: aberrations, beasts, celestials, constructs, dragons, elementals, fey, fiends, giants, monstrosities, oozes, plants, or undead. You have advantage on Survival checks to track and on Intelligence checks to recall information about your favored enemy type.

level1Features.name: Natural Explorer
level1Features.description: Choose a type of favored terrain. When you make an Intelligence or Wisdom check related to that terrain, your proficiency bonus is doubled. While traveling through your favored terrain your group cannot become lost, difficult terrain doesn't slow the group, and you can move stealthily at a normal pace.

subclasses.name: Hunter
subclasses.description: Emulating the Hunter archetype means accepting your place as a bulwark between civilization and the terrors of the wilderness.

features.name: Hunter's Prey
features.description: Choose one: Colossus Slayer (once per turn, deal extra 1d8 damage to a creature below its max HP), Giant Killer (react to a Large or larger creature's attack to make a melee attack against it), or Horde Breaker (once per turn, make an extra attack against a different adjacent foe).

subclasses.name: Beast Master
subclasses.description: The Beast Master archetype embodies a friendship between the civilized races and the beasts of the world.

features.name: Ranger's Companion
features.description: You gain a beast companion that accompanies you on adventures. It obeys your commands and acts on your initiative. You can use a bonus action to command the beast to take one of the actions in its stat block.

startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: Leather armor, two daggers, a shortbow, and 20 arrows
Option B: Leather armor, a rapier, and a shortsword
Option C: 100 GP

level1Features.name: Expertise
level1Features.description: Choose two of your skill proficiencies or one skill proficiency and your thieves' tools proficiency. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check using those skills.

level1Features.name: Sneak Attack
level1Features.description: Once per turn, deal an extra 1d6 damage when you hit a creature with a finesse or ranged weapon while you have advantage on the attack, or while an ally is adjacent to the target and you don't have disadvantage.

level1Features.name: Thieves' Cant
level1Features.description: You have learned the secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code used by thieves. It takes four times as long to convey a message compared to Common. You also understand a set of secret signs and symbols used to convey short messages.

subclasses.name: Thief
subclasses.description: You hone your skills in the larcenous arts. Burglars, bandits, cutpurses, and other criminals typically follow this archetype.

features.name: Fast Hands
features.description: You can use the bonus action granted by your Cunning Action to make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check, use your thieves' tools to disarm a trap or open a lock, or take the Use an Object action.

features.name: Second-Story Work
features.description: You gain the ability to climb faster than normal; climbing no longer costs extra movement. When you make a running jump, the distance you cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Dexterity modifier.

subclasses.name: Assassin
subclasses.description: You focus your training on the grim art of death. Those who adhere to this archetype are diverse — hired killers, spies, bounty hunters, and even sanctioned assassins.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with the disguise kit and the poisoner's kit.

features.name: Assassinate
features.description: During the first round of combat, you have advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn. Any hit you score against a surprised creature is a critical hit.

subclasses.name: Arcane Trickster
subclasses.description: Some rogues enhance their fine-honed skills of stealth and agility with magic, learning tricks of enchantment and illusion.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You learn three wizard cantrips (at least two from Enchantment or Illusion) and know 3 spells of 1st level, mostly from Enchantment and Illusion schools, using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability.

features.name: Mage Hand Legerdemain
features.description: You can make your Mage Hand invisible, and you can perform additional tasks: stow/retrieve objects, use thieves' tools, perform sleight of hand — all using Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) vs. the target's passive Perception.

startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: A light crossbow and 20 bolts, and an arcane focus
Option B: Two daggers and an arcane focus
Option C: 50 GP

level1Features.name: Sorcerous Origin
level1Features.description: Choose a sorcerous origin, which describes the source of your innate magical power. Your origin grants you features at 1st level and again at 6th, 14th, and 18th level.

level1Features.name: Spellcasting
level1Features.description: You can cast sorcerer spells using Charisma as your spellcasting ability. You know 4 spells and 4 cantrips at level 1. Unlike other spellcasters you know a fixed list of spells and do not prepare them daily.

level1Features.name: Sorcery Points
level1Features.description: You have 2 sorcery points at level 1, which you regain on a long rest. You can use sorcery points to create additional spell slots or empower your spells using Metamagic (unlocked at level 3).

subclasses.name: Draconic Bloodline
subclasses.description: Your innate magic comes from draconic magic that was mingled with your blood or that of your ancestors.

features.name: Dragon Ancestor
features.description: Choose a dragon type (black, blue, brass, bronze, copper, gold, green, red, silver, or white). You speak Draconic and gain advantage on Charisma checks when interacting with dragons of that type.

features.name: Draconic Resilience
features.description: Your hit point maximum increases by 1 per sorcerer level. When not wearing armor, your AC equals 13 + your Dexterity modifier.

subclasses.name: Wild Magic Surge
subclasses.description: Your innate magic comes from the wild forces of chaos that underlie the order of creation.

features.name: Wild Magic Surge
features.description: After you cast a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, the DM can have you roll a d20. On a 1, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table to determine a random magical effect.

features.name: Tides of Chaos
features.description: Once per long rest, gain advantage on one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. The DM can force you to make a Wild Magic Surge before you regain this feature.

startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: Leather armor, a light crossbow, 20 bolts, and an arcane focus
Option B: Leather armor, a dagger, and an arcane focus
Option C: 100 GP

level1Features.name: Otherworldly Patron
level1Features.description: You have struck a bargain with an otherworldly being of power. Your choice of patron grants you features at 1st level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th level.

level1Features.name: Pact Magic
level1Features.description: You cast warlock spells using Charisma as your spellcasting ability. You know 2 spells and 2 cantrips at level 1. Warlock spell slots are limited (1 slot at levels 1–2) but recover on a short or long rest. All your spell slots are the same level.

subclasses.name: The Archfey
subclasses.description: Your patron is a lord or lady of the fey, a creature of legend who holds secrets that were forgotten before the mortal races were born.

features.name: Fey Presence
features.description: As an action, fill a 10-foot cube centered on yourself with fey magic. Each creature in that area must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC = your spell save DC) or be charmed or frightened by you (your choice) until the end of your next turn. Usable once per short or long rest.

subclasses.name: The Fiend
subclasses.description: You have made a pact with a fiend from the lower planes of existence, a being whose aims are evil, even if you strive against those goals.

features.name: Dark One's Blessing
features.description: When you reduce a hostile creature to 0 HP, you gain temporary hit points equal to your Charisma modifier + your warlock level (minimum 1). These replace any existing temporary hit points.

subclasses.name: The Great Old One
subclasses.description: Your patron is a mysterious entity whose nature is utterly foreign to the fabric of reality. It might be associated with the Far Realm, or it could be an elder god.

features.name: Awakened Mind
features.description: You can telepathically speak to any creature you can see within 30 feet. You don't need to share a language, but the creature must understand at least one language. This telepathy is one-way.

startingEquipmentPackages.label: Option A: A quarterstaff and a spellbook
Option B: A dagger and a spellbook
Option C: 55 GP

level1Features.name: Arcane Recovery
level1Features.description: Once per day when you finish a short rest, choose expended spell slots to recover. The slots can have a combined level no greater than half your wizard level (rounded up). Slots of 6th level and higher cannot be recovered this way.

level1Features.name: Spellcasting
level1Features.description: You cast wizard spells using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability. Your spellbook contains 6 1st-level spells at character creation. You prepare a number of spells equal to your Intelligence modifier + your wizard level after each long rest.

subclasses.name: School of Evocation
subclasses.description: You focus your study on magic that creates powerful elemental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid.

features.name: Evocation Savant
features.description: The gold and time you spend to copy an evocation spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Sculpt Spells
features.description: When you cast an evocation spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to 1 + the spell's level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a successful save.

subclasses.name: School of Abjuration
subclasses.description: The School of Abjuration emphasizes magic that blocks, banishes, or protects. Its practitioners are sought when powerful undead or demons threaten.

features.name: Abjuration Savant
features.description: The gold and time you spend to copy an abjuration spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Arcane Ward
features.description: When you cast an abjuration spell of 1st level or higher, you can simultaneously use a strand of the spell's magic to create a magical ward on yourself. The ward has HP equal to twice your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier and absorbs damage you take.

subclasses.name: School of Conjuration
subclasses.description: As a conjurer, you favor spells that produce objects and creatures out of thin air.

features.name: Conjuration Savant
features.description: The gold and time you spend to copy a conjuration spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Minor Conjuration
features.description: As an action, you can conjure up an inanimate object in your hand or on the ground in an unoccupied space within 10 feet. The object can be no larger than 3 feet on a side and weigh no more than 10 pounds, and its form must be that of a nonmagical object.

subclasses.name: School of Divination
subclasses.description: The counsel of a diviner is sought by royalty and commoners alike, for all seek a clearer understanding of the past, present, and future.

features.name: Divination Savant
features.description: The gold and time you spend to copy a divination spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Portent
features.description: After a long rest, roll two d20s and record the numbers. Once before your next long rest, you can replace any attack roll, saving throw, or ability check made by you or a creature you can see with one of these rolls.

subclasses.name: School of Enchantment
subclasses.description: As a member of the School of Enchantment, you have honed your ability to magically entrance and beguile other people and monsters.

features.name: Enchantment Savant
features.description: The gold and time you spend to copy an enchantment spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Hypnotic Gaze
features.description: As an action, choose a creature within 5 feet and fix your gaze upon it. The creature must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it is charmed and incapacitated until the start of your next turn, its speed drops to 0, and it is visually dazed.

subclasses.name: School of Illusion
subclasses.description: You focus your studies on magic that dazzles the senses, befuddles the mind, and tricks even the wisest folk.

features.name: Illusion Savant
features.description: The gold and time you spend to copy an illusion spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Improved Minor Illusion
features.description: You know the Minor Illusion cantrip. When you cast it, you can create both a sound and an image with a single casting.

subclasses.name: School of Necromancy
subclasses.description: The School of Necromancy explores the cosmic forces of life, death, and undeath.

features.name: Necromancy Savant
features.description: The gold and time you spend to copy a necromancy spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Grim Harvest
features.description: Once per turn when you kill one or more creatures with a spell of 1st level or higher, you regain hit points equal to twice the spell's level, or three times if the spell is a necromancy spell.

subclasses.name: School of Transmutation
subclasses.description: You are a student of spells that modify energy and matter. To you, the world is not a fixed thing, but eminently mutable.

features.name: Transmutation Savant
features.description: The gold and time you spend to copy a transmutation spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Minor Alchemy
features.description: You can temporarily alter the physical properties of one nonmagical object, changing it from one substance into another. You perform a special alchemical procedure on one object composed entirely of wood, stone, iron, copper, or silver, transforming it into a different one of those materials.
